tools.name: contact_upsert
tools.description: Create or update a contact in the relationship graph. Use this to track people the user interacts with across channels (email, Slack, etc.).

id.description: Contact ID to update. Omit to create a new contact (or auto-match by channel address).

notes.description: Free-text notes about this contact (e.g. relationship, communication preferences, response expectations)

address.description: Channel address (email address, Slack handle, phone number, etc.)

is_primary.description: Whether this is the primary channel for this type

activity.description: Brief non-technical explanation of why this tool is being called

tools.name: contact_search
tools.description: Search for contacts by name, channel address, or other criteria. Returns matching contacts with their channel information.

channel_address.description: Search by channel address (email, phone, handle — partial match)

channel_type.description: Filter by channel type when searching by address (email, slack, whatsapp, phone, etc.)

activity.description: Brief non-technical explanation of why this tool is being called

tools.name: contact_merge
tools.description: Merge two contacts when you discover they are the same person (e.g. same person on email and Slack). Combines channels, merges notes, and deletes the donor contact.

keep_id.description: ID of the contact to keep (the surviving contact)

merge_id.description: ID of the contact to merge into the kept contact (will be deleted)

activity.description: Brief non-technical explanation of why this tool is being called

tools.name: google_contacts
tools.description: List or search Google Contacts. Requires contacts.readonly scope (re-authorization may be needed).

page_size.description: Number of contacts to return (default 50, for list)

activity.description: Brief non-technical explanation of why this tool is being called

account.description: Email address of the Gmail account to use. Required when multiple Gmail accounts are connected. If omitted, uses the most recently connected account.

tools.name: contact_message
tools.description: Send a message to an assistant contact via assistant-to-assistant (A2A) messaging. If the target isn't a contact yet, provide gateway_url and assistant_id to automatically initiate pairing — the user then submits the verification code via contact_pair_verify before the message can be sent.

contact_id.description: ID of the target assistant contact. Use this when the contact ID is already known.

contact_name.description: Display name of the target assistant contact. Used for lookup when contact_id is not provided. If multiple matches are found, the tool will return the list for disambiguation.

message.description: The message content to send to the target assistant

gateway_url.description: The gateway URL of the target assistant (e.g. https://gateway.example.com). Used to auto-initiate pairing when the target is not yet a contact.

assistant_id.description: The assistant ID of the target assistant. Used together with gateway_url to auto-initiate pairing when the target is not yet a contact.

activity.description: Brief non-technical explanation of why this tool is being called

tools.name: contact_pair
tools.description: Initiate A2A pairing with a remote assistant. Sends a pairing request to the target assistant's gateway. The target's guardian must approve and share a 6-digit verification code.

gateway_url.description: The gateway URL of the target assistant (e.g. https://gateway.example.com)

assistant_id.description: The assistant ID of the target assistant to pair with

activity.description: Brief non-technical explanation of why this tool is being called

tools.name: contact_pair_verify
tools.description: Complete A2A pairing by sending the 6-digit verification code received from the target assistant's guardian. Must have an active outbound pairing request (initiated via contact_pair or automatically by contact_message when the target wasn't a contact yet).

code.description: The 6-digit verification code shared by the target assistant's guardian

activity.description: Brief non-technical explanation of why this tool is being called
